component.name: Security Center
component.summary: Manage your system security, firewall and services

description.p: Security Center is a modern application for managing system security on Linux. It provides a comprehensive and user-friendly interface for controlling your firewall, monitoring network exposure, and managing system services.

ul.li: Firewall management with firewalld integration
Open and block ports with custom rules
Enable and disable network services
Configure firewall zones
Monitor network exposure and listening ports
Quick actions for common administrative tasks
System services management via systemd
Real-time traffic and connection statistics
Dependency security updates — patched bytes, quinn-proto, rustls-webpki, and rand vulnerabilities
Input validation hardening — centralized validators for protocols, port names, systemctl actions, and service names
Firewalld rich rule protection — protocols are validated before constructing any rich rule string
Config file sanitization — JSON files are validated, size-limited, and invalid entries are discarded on load
pkexec parameter validation — all privileged commands validate action and service names against allowlists
Semver version parsing — replaced custom version parser with robust semver comparison
Build script hardening — removed unwrap from build.rs environment variable reads
CI/CD pipeline — added GitHub Actions workflow with cargo audit, cargo deny, clippy, and tests
Test coverage expansion — increased from 5 to 31 tests covering security-critical paths
Accessibility improvements — added tooltip descriptions to chart widgets
Logging safety — default log level set to info; path leakage removed from log messages
Unused dependency cleanup — removed futures, once_cell, thiserror, and toml
Cross-distro support — works on Fedora, Ubuntu, Debian and all desktop environments
Fixed Debian/Ubuntu package dependencies (polkit package naming)
KDE Plasma compatibility — accent color detection no longer crashes on non-GNOME desktops
Config directory renamed from gnome-security-center to security-center (auto-migrated)
Universal autostart — desktop entry works on GNOME, KDE, XFCE and other DEs
AppImage now bundles GTK4/libadwaita shared libraries for portability
AppImage build script detects system architecture automatically
Clear error message when PolicyKit (pkexec) is not installed
Consolidated port view — same-port entries grouped into single rows
Improved firewall state display with three states (Active, Panic Mode, Inactive)
Traffic switch is disabled when firewall is stopped to prevent errors
Dashboard status now syncs correctly after Quick Actions changes
Fixed restart button stretching on the dashboard
Eliminated signal feedback loops causing error spam

description.p: Initial release of Security Center with the following features:

ul.li: Firewall status overview and quick toggle
Port management (open, block, remove rules)
Service management for firewalld services
Zone configuration and management
Network exposure monitoring
Quick administrative actions
System services management
Traffic and connection statistics with charts
